GenAiPromptTemplate.description: Classifies a Medical Insight into a semantic intent + fine-grained sub-topics so similar insights can be matched on shared intent and specific themes, not only subject tags.

templateVersions.content:
You are classifying a Medical Insight logged by a pharmaceutical field rep so that semantically similar insights can be grouped together.
Insight title: {!$Input:InsightTitle} Insight content: {!$Input:InsightContent} Assigned subjects: {!$Input:Subjects}
Step 1: Choose exactly ONE intent from this taxonomy: - Trial Access Barrier - Patient Education Gap - Off-Label Information Request - Safety Signal - Efficacy Question - Dosing or Administration - Reimbursement or Access - Competitive or Comparative - Guideline or Protocol - Other
Step 2: Identify 2-4 fine-grained sub-topics that describe what this insight is SPECIFICALLY about within the chosen intent. Use lowercase-hyphen style (e.g., "site-availability", "geographic-access"). Be specific. Generic tags like "general-question" are not useful. Pick sub-topics that would let you group this insight with other insights making the same specific point — not every insight in the same intent.
Good examples of sub-topics: - Trial Access Barrier: site-availability, geographic-access, eligibility-criteria, enrollment-logistics, trial-financial - Patient Education Gap: language-access, consent-literacy, caregiver-materials, side-effect-education, disease-state-education - Safety Signal: hematologic-toxicity, infusion-reaction, dermatologic, gi-toxicity, organ-toxicity, discontinuation - Reimbursement or Access: prior-authorization, copay-assistance, payer-denial, medicare-medicaid - Dosing or Administration: dose-modification, administration-route, dosing-schedule, renal-hepatic-adjustment
Step 3: Provide a confidence between 0 and 1 and a one-sentence rationale.
Return ONLY a JSON object with this exact shape and nothing else: {"intent": "<one value from the taxonomy>", "subtopics": ["<tag>","<tag>"], "confidence": <number 0-1>, "rationale": "<one sentence>"}
